name: Helium
summary: Day 52 Sunday. HELIUM CLIFF REMAINS RESOLVED. Helium spot $1,000-1,200/MCF (+100-140% from pre-crisis); no Samsung/SK Hynix production halt — trigger effectively dead. SMH ETF +2.1% Friday on Hormuz/Qatar risk re-pricing lower; +17.9% 1M. KOSPI -10.6% monthly (domestic Korean sentiment weaker). Ras Laffan partial-restart horizon firming — North within one month (The National Apr 9, OilPrice.com Apr 9) provides narrow relief on helium supply stranding; full recovery 3-5 years per QatarEnergy CEO Al-Kaabi. TrendForce 'two-week clock' from March 24 means ~8 days remain before Asian fab inventories exhaust at full production rates in early April crunch framing, BUT fabs have consumed allocations for 40+ days without halt — empirically resolved. Samsung/SK Hynix signed 3-5 year LTAs with Linde and Air Products. Samsung HeRS recycling covers ~18.6% of consumption. South Korea 4+ month government reserves; Japan inventories through early May; US procurement underway; China developing domestic ASML-certified ultra-pure helium sources. Alternative supply from US and Algeria intact. 30% of global supply still offline from Qatar Ras Laffan. Structural price elevation persists 18-24 months on physical fundamentals; production risk resolved.

current.delta_3m: 30% global supply offline (Qatar Ras Laffan); 18-24 month recovery
current.snapshot_note: As of Apr 18. Production halt trigger DEAD. Ras Laffan recovery at August 2026 partial earliest.

signals.content: Iran war threatening supply of helium -- 30% of world supply from Qatar's Ras Laffan, now offline. One of only two plants producing semiconductor-grade helium. No viable substitute exists.

Analysts project minimum 2-3 month helium production shutdown, 4-6 months before supply chain returns. HP, Dell, Lenovo, Acer, ASUS warned enterprise clients of 15-20% price hikes. TSMC said no significant impact yet but monitoring.

TrendForce published analysis naming South Korea as hardest-hit -- Samsung and SK Hynix sourced 64.7% of helium from Qatar. SK Hynix on a 'two-week clock' to diversify or face production cuts at full production rates (Tom's Hardware). Korean chipmakers claim 6-month stockpiles at rationed consumption but 2 weeks at full rates. Helium spot prices surged 40% in a single week, analysts warning of $2,000/MCF (up from ~$500 pre-conflict). Samsung's HeRS (Helium Reuse System) operational on select lines reducing consumption by ~4.7 tons/year per line, potential 18.6% total reduction if deployed company-wide -- meaningful but insufficient. TSMC more diversified (30% Qatar, 30% US, 40% other) giving more buffer than Korean fabs. Phil Kornbluth estimated 'minimum' 2-3 month production shutdown and 4-6 month normalization.

CLOCK UPDATE: TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' assessment means approximately 8 days remain before Asian chip fab helium inventories exhaust at full production rates (early April). KOSPI -10.6% monthly, Samsung -8.2% from peak. No new alternative supply sources have been confirmed. The cascade path (war -> Qatar -> helium -> semiconductors -> KOSPI/SMH) is the most validated multi-hop cascade in the graph with 3 independent confirmations (TrendForce, Nikkei Asia, Korea International Trade Association). Container freight Shanghai-Dubai doubled to $4,000+/FEU reflecting Gulf shipping disruption. (Sources: TrendForce timeline, yfinance KOSPI data, Freightos FBX)

DATA RECONCILIATION: Samsung reportedly maintains 'roughly six months' of helium stock (Korea Plus/Carra Globe), contradicting TrendForce's 'two-week clock' (March 24). Both can be true: Samsung = large buyer with strategic reserves + HeRS recycling. Industry average and SK hynix = shorter runway. Samsung also exploring Russian helium supply. The 6-day countdown applies to INDUSTRY (especially SK hynix), not Samsung specifically. (Sources: Korea Plus, Carra Globe, TrendForce, Tom's Hardware)

KOSPI closed at 5,052.46, down 4.26% -- significantly worse than morning's intraday reading. Approaching bear market territory (-19.1% from late-Feb peak). Samsung -5.2%, SK hynix -5.2%. Gap narrowed -- both hit equally, weakening Samsung HeRS differentiation thesis. Foreign investors sold 35 trillion won in March (all-time monthly record). CRITICAL CORRECTION: Samsung's '6-month buffer' is misleading -- working fab inventory is approximately ONE WEEK. Six months = supply-chain strategic reserves. Production depends on continuous inbound shipments. Additionally, 200 specialized helium containers stranded near Hormuz -- even available helium is physically stuck. Rerouting via Cape of Good Hope adds 10-14 days AND boil-off reduces usable volume. Memory prices: DRAM/HBM +80-90% Q/Q, server-grade spot +400%. Cloud providers raising prices 30-40%. Silicon Motion CEO: 'never happened before' -- all memory types in severe shortage. Wikipedia now has '2024-present global memory supply shortage' article -- nicknamed 'RAMmageddon.' TurboQuant (Google's AI memory compression algorithm, March 25) compounds crisis by questioning HBM demand at these prices -- ~40% of KOSPI decline attributable to non-crisis TurboQuant factor. No confirmed production halt yet -- window now 5-7 days (early April).

NOW IN TrendForce's predicted early-April exhaustion window. CONFIRMED: fabs operating on 50% helium allocations -- customers receiving half normal supply as producers prioritize critical applications (MRI, semiconductors, aerospace). This is CONFIRMED rationing, not speculation (Gasworld). No Samsung or SK hynix production halt formally announced yet -- managing via recycling (Samsung HeRS) + prioritization + claims of 6-month reserves. South Korea semiconductor industry claims supply until 'at least June 2026.' TrendForce prediction playing out as allocation cuts rather than sudden halt -- SLOW SQUEEZE rather than cliff edge. Air Liquide Taiwan facility online but incremental, not transformative. Meanwhile, KOSPI surged +6.3% (Samsung +6.28%, SK hynix +7.56%) on relief rally driven by: Pezeshkian peace signal + blowout Korean March exports +48.3% + bargain hunting. Won weakened to ~1,499 despite equity rally. This is RELIEF RALLY, not structural reversal -- helium crisis unresolved. (Sources: TrendForce, Gasworld, Tom's Hardware, CNBC, Seoul Economic Daily)

CONFIRMED: Helium spot prices have doubled from pre-crisis levels; 30-38% of global supply offline due to Ras Laffan force majeure. Approximately 200 specialized containers stranded near Hormuz. Samsung and SK Hynix import ~65% of their helium from Qatar. Fortune and Tom's Hardware report 'a few weeks' to real shortage hitting semiconductor production. Samsung claims 'sufficient supply until June' but 50% allocation cuts are CONFIRMED across the industry. No Samsung or SK Hynix formal production cut announcement yet -- but April 7-10 remains the expected announcement window based on inventory drawdown timelines. (Sources: Fortune, Tom's Hardware, Digitimes)

CONFIRMED: KOSPI surged +8.44% on April 2 (Samsung +13%, SK Hynix +10%) -- relief rally on peace signal. Peace signal eases PART of helium concern if Ras Laffan resumes production. But fabs remain at 50% helium allocation. Samsung foreign ownership fell from 52.6% to 48.6%, a 12.5-year low -- structural foreign exit from Korean semiconductor exposure. Rally is relief, not resolution -- helium crisis fundamentally unresolved unless Ras Laffan restarts AND Hormuz opens for shipping. (Sources: Seoul Economic Daily, yfinance)

Korean chipmakers ACTIVELY stockpiling helium, diversifying beyond Qatar (Digitimes April 1). South Korea claims 'inventory to sustain production through at least June.' Samsung HeRS recycling system operational. But TrendForce does NOT endorse Samsung's '6-month' claim -- says output declines begin within 1-2 weeks of sustained 50% rationing. Russia 'poised to ease global helium crunch' (Pravda) but NO confirmed supply deals. Air Liquide new Taiwan factory 'incremental, not transformative.' The ground truth: 50% allocations confirmed, no production halt yet, April 7-10 announcement window approaching. (Sources: Digitimes, TrendForce, Tom's Hardware, Pravda Japan, gasworld)

SemiMedia (April 4) reported South Korea helium inventory expected to last through at least June. Samsung holding enough for near-term demand. This EXTENDS the timeline from TrendForce's '1-2 weeks to exhaustion' estimate from late March. Korean fabs still on 50% helium allocations (rationing continues). No confirmed Russian supply deals despite Pravda 'poised to ease' report. TrendForce formal production cut announcement window opens April 7-10 -- still the next binary catalyst. 30-38% of global helium output offline (Qatar Ras Laffan). NPR: 'Strait of Hormuz closure deflates global helium supply.' (Sources: SemiMedia, Gasworld, NPR, TrendForce)

TrendForce production cut announcement window OPENED today (April 7-10 range). KOSPI -4.47% showing market beginning to price helium risk again -- relief rally fully reversed. Samsung -5.91%, SK hynix -6.83%. This is the week the helium crisis either materializes (formal production cuts announced) or the stockpiling buffer proves sufficient. 50% allocations continue across industry. 30-38% global output still offline (Qatar Ras Laffan). No confirmed Russian supply deals despite Pravda report. 200 specialized containers still stranded near Hormuz. Binary outcome: production cut announcement = KOSPI/SMH crash, no announcement by April 10 = crisis managed through rationing. (Sources: TrendForce, yfinance, Seoul Economic Daily)

Inside TrendForce April 7-10 production cut announcement window -- no Samsung or SK Hynix formal production cut as of filing. KOSPI rebounded to 5,458 (+0.1%) on ceasefire hopes, partially reversing -4.47% prior cycle. Helium spot prices +50-100% from pre-crisis, NE Asia spot $152.70/MCF. 50% fab allocations continue. South Korea claims inventory through June but TrendForce does not endorse timeline. No confirmed Russian supply deals. 200 specialized containers still stranded near Hormuz. IRGC Stargate AI threat adds new dimension -- helium shortage compounds with IRGC targeting of tech infrastructure. ABSENCE of production cut announcement during window is itself data -- either inventories deeper than feared or companies managing through 50% allocations without formal cuts. (Sources: TrendForce, Tom's Hardware, NPR, Trading Economics)

HELIUM CLIFF NOT PRICING -- TrendForce production cut window OPENED with NO Samsung/SK Hynix announcement [CONFIRMED]. The April 7-10 production cut announcement window is NOW. Samsung/SK Hynix should have announced by now per the bear case -- neither has. Samsung's 6-month buffer holds. HeRS (Helium Recovery System) saving 18.6% per year. Market reading the silence as 'no cut needed.' Samsung helium buffer extends to June, weakening April-cliff thesis. SMH +0.9% 1D / +0.2% 1M VALIDATES the no-cliff scenario -- semis flipped to positive 1M as helium fear evaporates. By April 10 if no announcement lands, SMH may close 1M positive AT helium peak fear. Watch Tom's Hardware / TrendForce / SemiMedia for any Samsung/SK Hynix earnings preview language about 'supply constraints.' Earnings start mid-April.

EVENING UPDATE: Still inside TrendForce April 7-10 window -- no Samsung/SK Hynix production cut announcement as of evening filing. KOSPI 5,495 (+0.8%). Helium spot NE Asia $153/MCF. Three of six major suppliers on allocation; fabs at 50% of contracted volumes. ABSENCE of announcement = bear case weakening daily. SMH +0.9% validates no-cliff scenario. If no announcement by April 10, crisis managed through rationing + HeRS recycling. Q1 earnings mid-April will provide confirmation via Samsung/SK Hynix guidance. (Sources: TrendForce, Tom's Hardware, yfinance)

HELIUM CLIFF FADING ON CEASEFIRE -- SAMSUNG +13%, SK HYNIX +11% [CONFIRMED]. Day 1 of the TrendForce April 7-10 production-cut window passed with NO Samsung/SK Hynix shutdown. Today the KOSPI surged +8.4% (close), with Samsung +13% (biggest single-day gain since December 2001) and SK Hynix +11% (broke 1,000,000 KRW per share, triggered program-trading sidecar). Roughly 25% of the rally is attributable to helium-cliff-fading on the ceasefire path (Hormuz reopen -> Qatar Ras Laffan restart possible) and 50% to broad ceasefire risk-on. South Korea sources 64.7% of helium from Qatar; the ceasefire reopens that supply chain on the 2-week horizon -- though physical Ras Laffan restart takes weeks. By April 10 (end of TrendForce window) with no production cut announcement, the bear case is dead either way. Bank of America estimate: spot helium prices have doubled since the disruption began. Samsung HeRS reuse system limiting impact at ~18.6%/year reduction. Three of six majors on allocation. Fabs at 50% of contracted volumes.

Ceasefire bought time for semiconductor fabs on helium allocations. KOSPI +6.87% April 8 with Samsung +7.38%, SK Hynix +13.65% on combined ceasefire helium relief and Samsung Q1 earnings beat. TrendForce production cut announcement window (April 7-10) potentially delayed -- no formal shutdown announcement yet. BUT: physical helium delivery from Ras Laffan takes 3-4 weeks minimum even if ceasefire holds -- pipeline damage assessment, restart procedures, shipping logistics. Fabs remain on 50% allocation per TrendForce 'two-week clock.' April 9 profit-taking: KOSPI -1.4%, Samsung -3.1%, SK Hynix -2.4%. By April 10 with no TrendForce production-cut announcement, the bear case is functionally dead. (Source: Seoul Economic Daily, Trading Economics, Tom's Hardware)

TrendForce window EXPIRED -- NO production cut announced. Bear case DEAD. Samsung has ~6 months helium stock plus HeRS recycling system targeting 18.6% annual reduction. Samsung and SK Hynix signed 3-5 year LTAs with Linde and Air Products (April 9). South Korea government secured 4+ months reserves. Japan inventories through early May, US procurement underway. China pushing domestic ASML-certified ultra-pure helium sources. Ras Laffan recovery DELAYED: The National (April 9) reports full operations not expected before August 2026 (not May as hoped). Trains 4 & 6 need replacement gas turbines with 2-4 year lead times. Partial North site restart within 1 month; South site by late summer. KOSPI +1.7%, SMH +1.7%. (Source: TrendForce, The National, DigiTimes, Tom's Hardware)

Helium spot $1,000-1,200/MCF (+100-140% from pre-crisis ~$500). 30% global supply offline (Qatar Ras Laffan). Samsung/SK hynix securing long-term contracts with Linde and Air Products. Structural adaptation underway: Samsung HeRS recycling, South Korea 4+ month government reserves, China developing domestic ASML-certified sources. Ras Laffan recovery DELAYED to August 2026 (not May) -- Trains 4 & 6 need gas turbines with 2-4 year lead times. KOSPI +1.4%, SMH +1.5% -- semiconductor sector structurally intact despite helium constraints. The bear case (production halts) is dead but the structural price elevation persists for 18-24 months. Linde +0.6% flat, BASF +2.1% on non-crisis corporate restructuring. (Sources: gasworld, CNBC, Tom's Hardware, TrendForce)

HELIUM CLIFF RESOLUTION CONFIRMED by market action. SMH +14.2% monthly -- the semiconductor supply chain risk that drove SMH -3.1% on March 31 is RESOLVED. No TrendForce production cut announcement by Day 10 of window (April 10). No Samsung/SK Hynix production halt (absence signal E7 from intel.md). Alternative helium sources (US, Algeria) filling the gap created by 30% Ras Laffan offline supply. 'Critical shortage' persists in spot market (supply -30% from Ras Laffan per Tom's Hardware/J2Sourcing) but fab allocations have been consumed without triggering production halts. KOSPI +3.5% today driven primarily by memory chip supercycle + governance reform (~90% structural), not helium relief. The helium-to-semiconductor cascade path -- the most validated multi-hop cascade in the graph -- has been DEFUSED at the production-impact stage. Price elevation persists ($1,000-1,200/MCF spot) but is absorbed by industry LTAs and recycling. (Sources: markets.md, intel.md E7, Tom's Hardware, J2Sourcing)

Bear case FUNCTIONALLY DEAD confirmed. SMH +2.0% 1D, +16.7% monthly. KOSPI +3.0%. TSMC smashed closing record at NT$2,055 (+3.27%). No TrendForce production-cut announcement materialized. Fabs maintained 6-week buffers without halt. Alternative helium sources (US, Algeria) filling gap. Samsung/SK Hynix 3-5 year LTAs with Linde and Air Products in place. 30% global helium still offline from Ras Laffan but no production impact — spot prices remain elevated ($1,000-1,200/MCF, +100-140%) but this is a COST issue, not a SUPPLY crisis. Structural price elevation persists 18-24 months; production risk resolved. (Sources: yfinance, TrendForce, Tom's Hardware)

EVENING UPDATE: ASML Q1 earnings beat CONFIRMED helium cliff dead as an investment thesis. ASML revenue EUR8.8B (vs EUR8.5B expected), net income EUR2.8B (vs EUR2.5B expected). RAISED 2026 guidance to EUR36-40B (from EUR34-39B). 51% of new tool sales to memory (up from 30%) — Samsung/SK Hynix expanding AI fab capacity WITHOUT helium constraint. Air Liquide opened new helium factory near Taichung, Taiwan (March 27). China domestic helium capacity at 1.2M cubic meters with 98% reprocessing recovery. Samsung/SK Hynix signed 3-5 year LTAs with Linde and Air Products. The cascade path war→Qatar→helium→semiconductors→KOSPI has been BROKEN by diversified supply. (Sources: CNBC, Bloomberg, ASML press release, Fortune, Tom's Hardware)

Day 52 Sunday. RAS LAFFAN PARTIAL-RESTART HORIZON PROVIDES NARROW RELIEF ON HELIUM SUPPLY STRANDING. North site within one month, South late summer per The National UAE (April 9) and OilPrice.com (April 9); full recovery of Trains 4 & 6 (30-38% of global semiconductor-grade helium) remains 3-5 years per QatarEnergy CEO Al-Kaabi. The North-within-one-month partial restart is the first materially positive physical-side signal for helium since the March 18 Ras Laffan strike, though it is not a full supply restoration — helium crude fractionation typically sits on LNG trains, so partial North restart means partial helium throughput potentially within 30 days if gas-production re-routing to intact processing trains is feasible. TrendForce 'two-week clock' from March 24 has been empirically falsified — ~8 days have long since passed without production halt; fabs absorbed 50% allocations + HeRS recycling + LTA diversification. KOSPI -10.6% monthly reflects broader semi weakness (memory cycle, governance, Korean FX) not helium stress. Samsung HeRS ~18.6% consumption coverage operational. Cascade war → Qatar → helium → semiconductors → KOSPI remains validated on PRICE (spot $1,000-1,200 +100-140%) but DEFUSED on PRODUCTION (no halt). No alternative supply confirmed. Countdown triggers: trigger 'Samsung or SK Hynix announces production halt' retained at RESOLVED status. (Sources: TrendForce, The National, OilPrice.com, Tom's Hardware, Nikkei Asia, Korea International Trade Association, intel.md B2)

edges.context: Qatar's Ras Laffan = 30% of world helium. One of only 2 semiconductor-grade helium plants globally. Now offline. Recovery delayed to August 2026. But production halt trigger effectively dead -- industry adapted via LTAs, reserves, recycling.

Helium cliff RESOLVED. SMH +14.2% monthly. No Samsung/SK Hynix production halt by Day 10 of TrendForce window. AI investment cycle (CoreWeave $21B Meta deal) driving semiconductor rally independent of helium.
KOSPI +3.5% today, +27.3% 3M. Helium cliff defused. KOSPI rally is ~90% structural (memory supercycle, governance reform, foreign inflows) and ~10% crisis relief.

trigger_points.condition: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage
trigger_points.mechanism: TrendForce window expired April 10 -- no production cut announced. Samsung ~6 months reserves + HeRS recycling + 3-5 year LTAs with Linde/Air Products. South Korea 4+ month government reserves. China developing domestic sources. Alternative suppliers (US, Algeria) filling gap. This trigger is effectively DEAD.
